description_long: This object consists of two large, vintage gasoline pumps and a smaller central pump, all showing significant weathering and rust. The pumps are primarily red with faded white panels and display the brand name 'CARLO' on round globes at the top of the larger units. Each large pump features a rectangular display area, a hose with a nozzle, and a metal base, while the smaller central pump is more compact with visible hoses and a simpler design. The surfaces are heavily worn, with chipped paint, rust stains, and visible grime, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The pumps have a robust, boxy construction typical of mid-20th-century gas stations, with metallic and glass components. No canopy, price board, or convenience store entrance is present, and the pumps are free-standing.
description: A set of three weathered, vintage red gasoline pumps with 'CARLO' branding, featuring rust, hoses, and prominent display globes.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large red pumps with 'CARLO' globes and a smaller central pump, all with visible hoses and display panels.
description_view_1: Side view of a single large pump, highlighting the hose, handle, and the rounded top globe, with visible rust and wear.
description_view_2: Rear angled view of the two large pumps and the smaller central pump, showing the backs and sides with more rust and faded paint.
description_view_3: Opposite side view of a single large pump, showing the back panel, rounded top, and additional weathering details.
attribute_fuel_pumps: Three visible fuel pumps: two large, vintage red pumps with 'CARLO' globes on top, each with a hose and nozzle, and one smaller central pump with exposed hoses. All pumps are heavily weathered, with no digital displays and prominent rust and paint wear.
attribute_station_signage: The only visible signage is the 'CARLO' branding on the round globes atop the two large pumps, in white letters on a red background. No other signs or notices are present.
